title: PR-204 Denial Code: Service Not Covered Under Your Plan — What to Do
meta_description: PR-204 means your insurance plan doesn't cover the service you received. Learn whether it was applied correctly, and what options you have even when coverage is excluded.
target_problem: Patient received a PR-204 denial indicating the service is not covered under their plan and needs to understand their options.
body:
PR-204 is a Claim Adjustment Reason Code meaning the service, equipment, or drug is not covered by the plan. It is a patient-responsibility code in the PR series, indicating that no insurance benefit applies.

Not all PR-204 denials are correct. Before accepting this denial, verify: was the service coded correctly; is the service actually listed as excluded in your plan documents; was a CO-50 medical necessity denial incorrectly coded as PR-204.

Options after a PR-204 denial: appeal the denial if the code was applied incorrectly; request a coverage exception if no covered alternative exists; check whether the service is required under federal guidelines for essential health benefits.

How to appeal: get your denial letter and EOB; review your Summary of Benefits and Coverage for the exclusion; contact your provider's billing office to confirm the procedure code; write your appeal letter; submit before the deadline (typically 180 days).

This content does not constitute legal or medical advice. MyAdvocate is not a law firm. For questions specific to your situation, consult a qualified attorney or healthcare professional.
